inviteSection.title: Invite Candidates
inviteSection.tooltip:
With an HTW email address you can invite students to register as a candidate. Invitations expire after 90 days and can be renewed at any time. The invitation authorizes registration.

Allowed formats:
• firstname.lastname@student.htw-berlin.de
• s0xxxxxx@htw-berlin.de

Bulk invite possible. Simply paste multiple emails into the field. Separators: semicolon (;), comma (,), space or line breaks. Excel columns can be pasted directly. Duplicates are ignored.

registeredList.title: Manage Candidates
registeredList.tooltip: Via 'Details' the profile and candidacies of the selected account are displayed on the right.

deleteModal.message: Are you sure you want to delete this list?
deleteModal.warning: One candidate has registered a candidacy for this list. The linked candidacy will also be permanently deleted.
deleteModal.warningPlural: {{count}} candidates have registered a candidacy for this list. The linked candidacies will also be permanently deleted.

questionManagement.title: Statement Management
questionManagement.subtitle: Here you can configure the statements that candidates and voters will position themselves on. Formulate the statements as neutrally as possible.

addNew.title: Create new statement
addNew.placeholder: Enter a neutrally formulated statement here...

questionManagement.noQuestions: No statements available yet. Start by adding a new statement.

deleteModal.title: Delete Statement
deleteModal.message: Are you sure you want to delete this statement?
deleteModal.warning: One candidate has already positioned themselves on this statement. This answer will be permanently deleted.
deleteModal.warningPlural: {{count}} persons already positioned themselves on this statement. These answers will be permanently deleted.
deleteModal.safeToDelete: This statement is still unanswered and can be safely deleted.

results.subtitle: Select your faculty and click on the individual committees to see your personal voting recommendations. You can adjust the number of displayed candidates as needed.

home.title: HTWahl-O-Mat
home.subtitle: With approximately 14,500 students, it is impossible to know all candidates. The HTWahl-O-Mat is a tool that can provide you with a voting orientation by comparing your positions on university issues with those of the candidates.
home.disclaimer: The HTWahl-O-Mat is not a voting recommendation, but an information service about elections and university politics.

warningModal.title: Unanswered Questions
warningModal.hintText: Only candidates who have positioned themselves against all statements will be displayed to voters in the HTWahl-O-Mat. However, you can still answer the missing questions later.

profile.title: Profile & Candidacies
profile.description: Here you can view your personal data and manage your candidacies for the upcoming elections. Click the 'Edit' button on the right to make adjustments.
profile.editModeDescription: You are in edit mode. Any changes (including to candidacies) will only be saved after you click on 'Save' at the bottom. With 'Cancel' all unsaved changes will be discarded.

personalData.title: Personal Data
personalData.aboutMePlaceholder: Tell the voters something about yourself...

candidacies.title: Candidacies
candidacies.noCandidacies: Please add at least one candidacy.
candidacies.noCandidaciesHint: You can do so by selecting a committee & list above and add with +.

hero.title: About the Elections at HTW Berlin
hero.subtitle: Here you will learn how the committee elections at HTW Berlin work and why your vote counts.

whatIsElected.title: What is being elected?
whatIsElected.description: In the committee elections, students elect their representatives in university bodies. These bodies participate in decisions concerning studies, teaching, organization, and student affairs.

howItWorks.title: How does the HTWahl-O-Mat work?
howItWorks.step1: Answer questions: You answer a series of questions about university politics.
howItWorks.step2: Compare positions: Your answers are compared with those of the candidates.
howItWorks.step3: Get results: You see which candidates best match your positions.

whyParticipate.title: Why is your participation important?
whyParticipate.reason1: More participation strengthens students' say in decision-making.
whyParticipate.reason2: Results become more meaningful and representative.
whyParticipate.reason3: You get a chance to actively shape your study environment.
